title: senecio sylvaticus subsp. viscidulus
intro: Senecio sylvaticus subsp. viscidulus is a subspecies of the perennial Senecio sylvaticus plant, also known as wood germander, which is native to the western Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a bitter tonic and aid in digestion, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Senecio sylvaticus subsp. viscidulus include its antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which can help to boost the immune system and protect against infections. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are often used to make teas and other remedies that can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
